title: KODO Capability Score
description: Single-line JSON emitted by kodo-capability.sh. This is the immutable ground truth evaluator consumed by kodo-autoevolve.sh. Capability measures how GOOD KODO is at its job over time — complementary to health_score, which measures whether KODO is currently WORKING. Schema changes require a version bump, an AUTOEVOLVE.md update, and operator review.

version.description: Schema version. Increment only when the capability formula changes.

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when kodo-capability.sh started its read.

capability_score.description: Unbounded scalar, normalized to 1.0 at Day 0 baseline, higher is better. Computed multiplicatively — any single factor approaching zero drags the whole score down. See AUTOEVOLVE.md §Evaluator for the exact formula.

mode.description: 'observed' = computed from live kodo.db over a 7-day window. 'simulated' = computed from the fixture harness replay. Autoevolve ratchets on 'simulated'; it validates against 'observed' on a weekly cadence.

baseline.description: Day-0 baselines, FROZEN at autoevolve initialization. These values never change unless the operator runs `kodo-autoevolve.sh --recapture-baseline`, which is a deliberate full reset of the ratchet.

current.description: Current-window measurements. Window is 7 days for observed mode, one fixture harness run for simulated mode.

automation_rate.description: clean_resolutions_in_window / total_events_in_window. 'Clean' means reached a terminal state without crossing into 'deferred' or triggering a Telegram alert.

incident_rate.description: From merge_outcome_stats_30d view — reverted+hotfixed / total. Slow signal.

alerts_per_event.description: Operator Telegram alerts emitted / total events processed. The 'how often does KODO bother a human' metric. Lower is better; 0 is ideal; values >1 are possible if a single event generates multiple alerts.

median_time_to_resolution_s.description: Median wall-clock seconds from pending→terminal for events resolved in window.

factors.description: Decomposition of the capability_score into its five multiplicative factors. Each factor is in [0, ∞); their product is capability_score. Exposed for debugging the ratchet — autoevolve can inspect which dimension regressed.

f_autonomy.description: = exp(−current.alerts_per_event). Exponential decay: one alert per event knocks this to ~0.37.

f_cost.description: = baseline.cost_per_resolution_usd / max(current.cost_per_resolution_usd, 0.01). Above 1.0 means cheaper than baseline. Capped at 10.0 in the evaluator to prevent a single zero-cost trial from dominating.

f_speed.description: = min(2.0, baseline.median_time_to_resolution_s / max(current.median_time_to_resolution_s, 1)). Capped at 2× baseline to prevent outlier-driven score explosions.

counts.description: Raw tallies used to compute factors. Autoevolve logs these for post-hoc trial debugging but does not use them in the ratchet formula.
